implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: Six Pydantic v2 entity models
AC-002: ASSUM-007 docstring on every pipeline-history entity_id
AC-003: ASSUM-008 ordering documented on SessionOutcome.gate_decision_ids
AC-005: redact_credentials() with documented regex set
AC-006: Unit tests for redact_credentials per-pattern + overlap + unicode + fuzz
AC-007: Validation tests for empty/missing required fields
AC-008: Black, ruff, and mypy all clean on the new files

completion_promises.criterion_text: All six entity models defined with Pydantic v2 BaseModel
completion_promises.evidence: Six models defined in src/forge/memory/models.py: GateDecision, CapabilityResolution, OverrideEvent, CalibrationAdjustment, SessionOutcome, CalibrationEvent. Each subclasses pydantic.BaseModel and uses ConfigDict(extra='forbid') (v2 surface, not v1 inner Config). Verified by tests/unit/test_models.py::TestPydanticV2BaseModel — 13 parametrised tests + an __all__ export check, all green.

completion_promises.criterion_text: entity_id field documented as 'MUST be sourced from SQLite UUID, never generated at write time' (ASSUM-007 resolution)
completion_promises.evidence: Every UUID-typed entity_id Field on GateDecision, CapabilityResolution, OverrideEvent, CalibrationAdjustment, and SessionOutcome carries the description 'MUST be sourced from SQLite UUID, never generated at write time (ASSUM-007 resolution).' Verified by TestEntityIdSourcedFromSqliteDocstring (parametrised over the five pipeline-history models) plus a separate UUID-annotation assertion.

completion_promises.criterion_text: gate_decision_ids on SessionOutcome documented as ordered ascending by decided_at (ASSUM-008 resolution)
completion_promises.evidence: SessionOutcome.gate_decision_ids carries Field(description=...) text that explicitly states 'The list MUST be sorted ascending by each referenced GateDecision.decided_at timestamp (ASSUM-008 resolution)'. Verified by TestSessionOutcomeOrdering::test_gate_decision_ids_field_documents_ordering plus round-trip and default-empty behavioural tests.

completion_promises.criterion_text: CalibrationEvent.entity_id is deterministic from (source_file, line_range_hash) so re-ingestion is idempotent
completion_promises.evidence: CalibrationEvent.entity_id is annotated as str (the deliberate exception to the UUID rule) and its description spells out the deterministic-id contract referencing both source_file and the line range hash. Verified by TestCalibrationEventDeterministicId across three tests: type annotation, description content, and an idempotent re-ingestion check (two events constructed with the same deterministic id must compare equal).

completion_promises.criterion_text: redact_credentials() function implemented with documented regex set
completion_promises.evidence: redact_credentials(text: str) -> str in src/forge/memory/redaction.py covers all required patterns: 'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82,}', 'ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}', 'ghs_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}', 'Bearer [A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,}', and word-bounded '[0-9a-fA-F]{40,}'. Patterns are ordered most-specific-first (the GitHub fine-grained PAT runs before the bearer regex so its '_' characters are not truncated). Each pattern's justification is documented in the module docstring; pattern justification coverage is asserted by TestPurityAndApi::test_module_docstring_documents_each_pattern.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests for redact_credentials() covering each pattern (positive + negative cases, including overlapping matches and unicode)
completion_promises.evidence: 31 redaction tests in tests/unit/test_redaction.py: 3 cases per pattern (positive/negative/edge) for Bearer, ghp_, ghs_, github_pat_, and long-hex; a 3-test fuzz suite (40+40+20 random hex blobs with a fixed seed _FUZZ_SEED for reproducibility) confirming redaction iff length>=40; 4 overlapping-match tests including 'github_pat then bearer no truncation', idempotent double application, and a union-of-credentials no-leakage check; 3 unicode tests covering Japanese/Arabic prose around tokens and emoji-adjacent hex; plus 6 purity/API tests including a caplog-based assertion that the function does not log the original.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests confirming model validation rejects empty/missing required fields
completion_promises.evidence: TestModelValidationRejectsBadInput exercises every model with pytest.raises(ValidationError): missing entity_id on GateDecision, empty stage_name, score outside [0,1]; empty capability on CapabilityResolution; missing gate_decision_id on OverrideEvent; missing approved on CalibrationAdjustment plus a positive companion that supersedes defaults to None; invalid outcome literal and empty build_id on SessionOutcome; empty entity_id and empty source_file on CalibrationEvent. Models use ConfigDict(extra='forbid') and Field(..., min_length=1) on must-be-non-empty strings.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran the project's documented formatter (Black, per .claude/rules/code-style.md: 'Use Black formatter, max line length 88') on src/forge/memory/ and tests/unit/. Three files were reformatted on this turn (test_models.py, test_redaction.py, redaction.py); after reformat, 'black --check src/forge/memory/ tests/unit/' reports '6 files would be left unchanged' — zero diffs. Also ran 'ruff check src/forge/memory/ tests/unit/' (default rule set) which reports 'All checks passed!' and 'mypy --explicit-package-bases src/forge/memory/' which reports 'Success: no issues found in 3 source files'. All 70 tests still pass after reformat (0.05s wall-clock). Coverage on forge.memory.* is 100.0% (79/79 statements).
